  1076→func containerMemoryFromBlock(blocks []memorymanagerstate.Block) []*podresourcesapi.ContainerMemory {
  1077→	var containerMemories []*podresourcesapi.ContainerMemory
  1078→
  1079→	for _, b := range blocks {
  1080→		containerMemory := podresourcesapi.ContainerMemory{
  1081→			MemoryType: string(b.Type),
  1082→			Size:       b.Size,
  1083→			Topology: &podresourcesapi.TopologyInfo{
  1084→				Nodes: []*podresourcesapi.NUMANode{},
  1085→			},
  1086→		}
  1087→
  1088→		for _, numaNodeID := range b.NUMAAffinity {
  1089→			containerMemory.Topology.Nodes = append(containerMemory.Topology.Nodes, &podresourcesapi.NUMANode{ID: int64(numaNodeID)})
  1090→		}
  1091→
  1092→		containerMemories = append(containerMemories, &containerMemory)
  1093→	}
  1094→
  1095→	return containerMemories
  1096→}

  1110→func (cm *containerManagerImpl) UpdateAllocatedResourcesStatus(pod *v1.Pod, status *v1.PodStatus) {
  1111→
  1112→	// For now we only support Device Plugin
  1113→	cm.deviceManager.UpdateAllocatedResourcesStatus(pod, status)
  1114→

{\n\t\t\terr = manager.Apply(pid)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to move PID %d (in %q) to %q: %v\", pid, cont, name, err))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Also apply oom-score-adj to processes\n\toomAdjuster := oom.NewOOMAdjuster()\n\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"Attempting to apply oom_score_adj to process\", \"oomScoreAdj\", oomScoreAdj, \"pid\", pid)\n\tif err := append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to apply oom score %d to PID %d: %v\", oomScoreAdj, pid, err))\n\t}\n\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n}\n\n// getContainer returns the cgroup associated with the specified pid.\n// It enforces a unified hierarchy for memory and cpu cgroups.\n// On systemd environments, it uses the name=systemd cgroup for the specified pid.\nfunc getContainer(logger klog.Logger, pid int) (string, error) {\n\tcgs, err := cgroups.ParseCgroupFile(fmt.Sprintf(\"/proc/%d/cgroup\", pid))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tif cgroups.IsCgroup2UnifiedMode() {\n\t\tc, found := cgs[\"\"]\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", cgroups.NewNotFoundError(\"unified\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn c, nil\n\t}\n\n\tcpu, found := cgs[\"cpu\"]\n\tif !found {\n\t\treturn \"\", cgroups.NewNotFoundError(\"cpu\")\n\t}\n\tmemory, found := cgs[\"memory\"]\n\tif !found {\n\t\treturn \"\", cgroups.NewNotFoundError(\"memory\")\n\t}\n\n\t// since we use this container for accounting, we need to ensure its a unified hierarchy.\n\tif cpu != memory {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"cpu and (pid 1, PIDs already in system & kubelet containers)\n\t\tpids := make([]int, 0, len(allPids))\n\t\tfor _, pid := range allPids {\n\t\t\tif pid == 1 || isKernelPid(pid) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpids = append(pids, pid)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if we have moved all the non-kernel PIDs.\n\t\tif len(pids) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlogger.V(3).Info(\"Moving non-kernel processes\", \"pids\", pids)\n\t\tfor _, pid := range pids {\n\t\t\terr := manager.Apply(pid)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tname := \"\"\n\t\t\t\tcgroups, err := manager.GetCgroups()\n\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tname = cgroups.Name\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfinalErr = fmt.Errorf(\"failed to move PID %d into the system container     31→func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PreCreateContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerConfig *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig) error {
    32→	if i.cpuManager != nil {
    33→		allocatedCPUs := i.cpuManager.GetCPUAffinity(string(pod.UID), container.Name)
    34→		if !allocatedCPUs.IsEmpty() {
    35→			containerConfig.Linux.Resources.CpusetCpus = allocatedCPUs.String()
    36→		}
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	if i.memoryManager != nil {
    40→		numaNodes := i.memoryManager.GetMemoryNUMANodes(logger, pod, container)
    41→		if numaNodes.Len() > 0 {
    42→			var affinity []string
    43→			for _, numaNode := range sets.List(numaNodes) {
    44→				affinity = append(affinity, strconv.Itoa(numaNode))
    45→			}
    46→			containerConfig.Linux.Resources.CpusetMems = strings.Join(affinity, ",")
    47→		}
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	return nil
    51→}

   174→	case PolicyNone:
   175→		policy, err = NewNonePolicy(cpuPolicyOptions)
   176→		if err != nil {
   177→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("new none policy error: %w", err)
   178→		}
   179→
   180→	case PolicyStatic:
   181→		logger.Info("Detected CPU topology", "topology", topo)
   182→
   183→		reservedCPUs, ok := nodeAllocatableReservation[v1.ResourceCPU]
   184→		if !ok {
   185→			// The static policy cannot initialize without this information.
   186→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("[cpumanager] unable to determine reserved CPU resources for static policy")
   187→		}
   188→		if reservedCPUs.IsZero() {
   189→			// The static policy requires this to be nonzero. Zero CPU reservation
   190→			// would allow the shared pool to be completely exhausted. At that point
   191→			// either we would violate our guarantee of exclusivity or need to evict
   192→			// any pod that has at least one container that requires zero CPUs.
   193→			// See the comments in policy_static.go for more details.
   194→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("[cpumanager] the static policy requires systemreserved.cpu + kubereserved.cpu to be greater than zero")
   195→		}
   196→
   197→		// Take the ceiling of the reservation, since fractional CPUs cannot be
   198→		// exclusively allocated.
   199→		reservedCPUsFloat := float64(reservedCPUs.MilliValue()) / 1000
   200→		numReservedCPUs := int(math.Ceil(reservedCPUsFloat))
   201→		policy, err = NewStaticPolicy(logger, topo, numReservedCPUs, specificCPUs, affinity, cpuPolicyOptions)
   202→		if err != nil {
   203→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("new static policy error: %w", err)
   204→		}
   205→
   206→	default:
   207→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown policy: \"%s\"", cpuPolicyName)
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	manager := &manager{
   211→		policy:                     policy,
   212→		reconcilePeriod:            reconcilePeriod,
   213→		lastUpdateState:            state.NewMemoryState(logger),
   214→		topology:                   topo,
   215→		nodeAllocatableReservation: nodeAllocatableReservation,
   216→		stateFileDirectory:         stateFileDirectory,
   217→		allCPUs:                    topo.CPUDetails.CPUs(),
   218→	}
   219→	manager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}
   220→	return manager, nil
   221→}
   222→
   223→func (m *manager) Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error {
   224→	logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   225→	logger.Info("Starting", "policy", m.policy.Name())
   226→	logger.Info("Reconciling", "reconcilePeriod", m.reconcilePeriod)
   227→	m.sourcesReady = sourcesReady
   228→	m.activePods = activePods
   229→	m.podStatusProvider = podStatusProvider
   230→	m.containerRuntime = containerRuntime
   231→	m.containerMap = initialContainers
   232→
   233→	stateImpl, err := state.NewCheckpointState(logger, m.stateFileDirectory, cpuManagerStateFileName, m.policy.Name(), m.containerMap)
   234→	if err != nil {
   235→		logger.Error(err, "Could not initialize checkpoint manager, please drain node and remove policy state file")
   236→		return err
   237→	}
   238→	m.state = stateImpl
   239→
   240→	err = m.policy.Start(logger, m.state)
   241→	if err != nil {
   242→		logger.Error(err, "Policy start error")
   243→		return err
   244→	}
   245→
   246→	logger.V(4).Info("CPU manager started", "policy", m.policy.Name())
   247→
   248→	m.allocatableCPUs = m.policy.GetAllocatableCPUs(m.state)
   249→
   250→	if m.policy.Name() == string(PolicyNone) {
   251→		return nil
   252→	}
   253→	// Periodically call m.reconcileState() to continue to keep the CPU sets of
   254→	// all pods in sync with and guaranteed CPUs handed out among them.
   255→	go wait.Until(func() { m.reconcileState(ctx) }, m.reconcilePeriod, wait.NeverStop)
   256→	return nil
   257→}
   258→
   259→func (m *manager) Allocate(p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container) error {
   260→	logger := klog.TODO() // until we move topology manager to contextual logging
   261→
   262→	// Garbage collect any stranded resources before allocating CPUs.
   263→	m.removeStaleState(logger)
   264→
   265→	m.Lock()
   266→	defer m.Unlock()
   267→
   268→	// Call down into the policy to assign this container CPUs if required.
   269→	err := m.policy.Allocate(logger, m.state, p, c)
   270→	if err != nil {
   271→		logger.Error(err, "policy error")
   272→		return err
   273→	}
   274→
   275→	return nil
   276→}
   277→
   278→func (m *manager) AddContainer(logger logr.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {
   279→	m.Lock()
   280→	defer m.Unlock()
   281→	if cset, exists := m.state.GetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name); exists {
   282→		m.lastUpdateState.SetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name, cset)
   283→	}
   284→	m.containerMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)
   285→	logger.V(4).Info("Added Container", "pod", klog.KObj(pod), "podUID", pod.UID, "containerName", container.Name, "containerID", containerID)
   286→}
   287→
   288→func (m *manager) RemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error {
   289→	m.Lock()
   290→	defer m.Unlock()
   291→
   292→	err := m.policyRemoveContainerByID(logger, containerID)
   293→	if err != nil {
   294→		logger.Error(err, "RemoveContainer error")
   295→		return err
   296→	}
   297→
   298→	return nil
   299→}

int(math.Ceil(reservedCPUsFloat))\n\t\tpolicy, err = NewStaticPolicy(logger, topo, numReservedCPUs, specificCPUs, affinity, cpuPolicyOptions)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"new policy,\n\t\treconcilePeriod:            reconcilePeriod,\n\t\tlastUpdateState:            state.NewMemoryState(logger),\n\t\ttopology:                   topo,\n\t\tnodeAllocatableReservation: nodeAllocatableReservation,\n\t\tstateFileDirectory:         stateFileDirectory,\n\t\tallCPUs:                    topo.CPUDetails.CPUs(),\n\t}\n\tmanager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}\n\treturn manager, nil\n}\n\nfunc (m *manager) Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error {\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tlogger.Info(\"Starting\", \"policy\", m.policy.Name())\n\tlogger.Info(\"Reconciling\",     17→package topologymanager
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"fmt"
    22→	"time"
    23→
    24→	cadvisorapi "github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1"
    25→	v1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    26→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    27→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask"
    28→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle"
    29→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics"
    30→)
    31→
    32→const (
    33→	// defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes specifies the maximum number of NUMA Nodes that
    34→	// the TopologyManager supports on the underlying machine.
    35→	//
    36→	// At present, having more than this number of NUMA Nodes will result in a
    37→	// state explosion when trying to enumerate possible NUMAAffinity masks and
    38→	// generate hints for them. As such, if more NUMA Nodes than this are
    39→	// present on a machine and the TopologyManager is enabled, an error will
    40→	// be returned and the TopologyManager will not be loaded.
    41→	defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes = 8
    42→	// ErrorTopologyAffinity represents the type for a TopologyAffinityError
    43→	ErrorTopologyAffinity = "TopologyAffinityError"
    44→)
    45→
    46→// TopologyAffinityError represents an resource alignment error
    47→type TopologyAffinityError struct{}
    48→
    49→func (e TopologyAffinityError) Error() string {
    50→	return "Resources cannot be allocated with Topology locality"
    51→}
    52→
    53→func (e TopologyAffinityError) Type() string {
    54→	return ErrorTopologyAffinity
    55→}
    56→
    57→// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod topology hints
    58→type Manager interface {
    59→	// PodAdmitHandler is implemented by Manager
    60→	lifecycle.PodAdmitHandler
    61→	// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider
    62→	// wants to be consulted with when making topology hints
    63→	AddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)
    64→	// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking
    65→	AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)
    66→	// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking
    67→	RemoveContainer(containerID string) error
    68→	// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints
    69→	Store
    70→}
    71→
    72→type manager struct {
    73→	//Topology Manager Scope
    74→	scope Scope
    75→}
    76→
    77→// HintProvider is an interface for components that want to collaborate to
    78→// achieve globally optimal concrete resource alignment with respect to
    79→// NUMA locality.
    80→type HintProvider interface {
    81→	// GetTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible
    82→	// concrete resource allocations in terms of NUMA locality hints. Each hint
    83→	// is optionally marked "preferred" and indicates the set of NUMA nodes
    84→	// involved in the hypothetical allocation. The topology manager calls
    85→	// this function for each hint provider, and merges the hints to produce
    86→	// a consensus "best" hint. The hint providers may subsequently query the
    87→	// topology manager to influence actual resource assignment.
    88→	GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint
    89→	// GetPodTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible
    90→	// concrete resource allocations per Pod in terms of NUMA locality hints.
    91→	GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint
    92→	// Allocate triggers resource allocation to occur on the HintProvider after
    93→	// all hints have been gathered and the aggregated Hint is available via a
    94→	// call to Store.GetAffinity().
    95→	Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error
    96→}
    97→
    98→// Store interface is to allow Hint Providers to retrieve pod affinity
    99→type Store interface {
   100→	GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint
   101→	GetPolicy() Policy
   102→}
   103→
   104→// TopologyHint is a struct containing the NUMANodeAffinity for a Container
   105→type TopologyHint struct {
   106→	NUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask
   107→	// Preferred is set to true when the NUMANodeAffinity encodes a preferred
   108→	// allocation for the Container. It is set to false otherwise.
   109→	Preferred bool
   110→}
   111→
   112→// IsEqual checks if TopologyHint are equal
   113→func (th *TopologyHint) IsEqual(topologyHint TopologyHint) bool {
   114→	if th.Preferred == topologyHint.Preferred {
   115→		if th.NUMANodeAffinity == nil || topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity == nil {
   116→			return th.NUMANodeAffinity == topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity
   117→		}
   118→		return th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity)
   119→	}
   120→	return false

As such, if more NUMA Nodes than this are\n\t// present on a machine and the TopologyManager is enabled, an error will\n\t// be returned and the TopologyManager will not be loaded.\n\tdefaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes = 8\n\t// ErrorTopologyAffinity represents the type for a TopologyAffinityError\n\tErrorTopologyAffinity = \"TopologyAffinityError\"\n)\n\n// TopologyAffinityError represents an resource alignment error\ntype TopologyAffinityError struct{}\n\nfunc (e TopologyAffinityError) Error() string {\n\treturn \"Resources cannot be allocated with Topology locality\"\n}\n\nfunc (e TopologyAffinityError) Type() string {\n\treturn ErrorTopologyAffinity\n}\n\n// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod topology hints\ntype Manager interface {\n\t// PodAdmitHandler is implemented by Manager\n\tlifecycle.PodAdmitHandler\n\t// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider\n\t// wants to be consulted with when making topology hints\n\tAddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)\n\t// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking\n\tAddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\n\t// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking\n\tRemoveContainer(containerID string) error\n\t// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints\n\tStore\n}\n\ntype manager struct {\n\t//Topology Manager Scope\n\tscope Scope\n}\n\n// HintProvider is an interface for components that want to collaborate to\n// achieve globally optimal concrete resource alignment with respect to\n// NUMA locality.\ntype HintProvider interface {\n\t// GetTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\n\t// concrete resource allocations in terms of NUMA locality hints. Each hint\n\t// is optionally marked \"preferred\" and indicates the set of NUMA nodes\n\t// involved in the hypothetical allocation. The topology manager calls\n\t// this function for each hint provider, and merges the hints to produce\n\t// a consensus \"best\" hint. The hint providers may subsequently query the\n\t// topology manager to influence actual resource assignment.\n\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint\n\t// GetPodTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\n\t// concrete resource allocations per Pod in terms of NUMA locality hints.\n\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint\n\t// Allocate triggers resource allocation to occur on the HintProvider after\n\t// all hints have been gathered and the aggregated Hint is available via a\n\t// call to Store.GetAffinity().\n\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\n}\n\n// Store interface is to allow Hint Providers to retrieve pod affinity\ntype Store interface {\n\tGetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint\n\tGetPolicy() Policy\n}\n\n// TopologyHint is a struct containing the NUMANodeAffinity for a Container\ntype TopologyHint struct {\n\tNUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask\n\t// Preferred is set to true when the NUMANodeAffinity encodes a preferred\n\t// allocation for the Container. It is set to false otherwise.\n\tPreferred bool\n}\n\n// IsEqual checks if TopologyHint are equal\nfunc (th *TopologyHint) IsEqual(topologyHint TopologyHint) bool {\n\tif th.Preferred == topologyHint.Preferred {\n\t\tif th.NUMANodeAffinity == nil || topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity == nil {\n\t\t\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity == topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity)\n\t}\n\treturn false", "numLines": 120, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 244}}

    56→// defaultReconcilePeriod is the default reconciliation period to keep all claim info state in sync.
    57→const defaultReconcilePeriod = 60 * time.Second
    58→
    59→// The time that DRA drivers have to come back after being unregistered
    60→// before the kubelet removes their ResourceSlices.
    61→//
    62→// This must be long enough to actually allow stopping a pod and
    63→// starting the replacement (otherwise ResourceSlices get deleted
    64→// unnecessarily) and not too long (otherwise the time window were
    65→// pods might still get scheduled to the node after removal of a
    66→// driver is too long).
    67→//
    68→// 30 seconds might be long enough for a simple container restart.
    69→// If a DRA driver wants to be sure that slices don't get wiped,
    70→// it should use rolling updates.
    71→const defaultWipingDelay = 30 * time.Second
    72→
    73→// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of pods to reconcile.
    74→type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod
    75→
    76→// GetNodeFunc is a function that returns the node object using the kubelet's node lister.
    77→type GetNodeFunc func(context.Context) (*v1.Node, error)

    80→// It ensures that they are prepared before starting pods

    45→// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of active pods

    47→
    48→type runtimeService interface {
    49→	UpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, id string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) error
    50→}
    51→
    52→type sourcesReadyStub struct{}
    53→
    54→func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}
    55→func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }
    56→
    57→// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod memory.
    58→type Manager interface {
    59→	// Start is called during Kubelet initialization.
    60→	Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error

    64→	AddContainer(logger klog.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)
    65→
    66→	// Allocate is called to pre-allocate memory resources during Pod admission.
    67→	// This must be called at some point prior to the AddContainer() call for a container, e.g. at pod admission time.
    68→	Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error
    69→
    70→	// RemoveContainer is called after Kubelet decides to kill or delete a
    71→	// container. After this call, any memory allocated to the container is freed.
    72→	RemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error
    73→
    74→	// State returns a read-only interface to the internal memory manager state.
    75→	State() state.Reader
    76→
    77→	// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface
    78→	// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this
    79→	// and other resource controllers.
    80→	GetTopologyHints(*v1.Pod, *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint

    42→	// PolicyStatic is the name of the static policy.
    43→	// Should options be given, these will be ignored and backward (up to 1.21 included)
    44→	// compatible behaviour will be enforced
    45→	PolicyStatic policyName = "static"
    46→	// ErrorSMTAlignment represents the type of an SMTAlignmentError
    47→	ErrorSMTAlignment = "SMTAlignmentError"
    48→)
    49→
    50→// SMTAlignmentError represents an error due to SMT alignment
    51→type SMTAlignmentError struct {
    52→	RequestedCPUs         int
    53→	CpusPerCore           int
    54→	AvailablePhysicalCPUs int
    55→	CausedByPhysicalCPUs  bool
    56→}
    57→
    58→func (e SMTAlignmentError) Error() string {
    59→	if e.CausedByPhysicalCPUs {
    60→		return fmt.Sprintf("SMT Alignment Error: not enough free physical CPUs: available physical CPUs = %d, requested CPUs = %d, CPUs per core = %d", e.AvailablePhysicalCPUs, e.RequestedCPUs, e.CpusPerCore)
    61→	}
    62→	return fmt.Sprintf("SMT Alignment Error: requested %d cpus not multiple cpus per core = %d", e.RequestedCPUs, e.CpusPerCore)
    63→}
    64→
    65→// Type returns human-readable type of this error. Used in the admission control to populate Admission Failure reason.
    66→func (e SMTAlignmentError) Type() string {
    67→	return ErrorSMTAlignment
    68→}
    69→
    70→// staticPolicy is a CPU manager policy that does not change CPU
    71→// assignments for exclusively pinned guaranteed containers after the main
    72→// container process starts.
    73→//
    74→// This policy allocates CPUs exclusively for a container if all the following
    75→// conditions are met:
    76→//
    77→// - The pod QoS class is Guaranteed.
    78→// - The CPU request is a positive integer.
    79→//
    80→// The static policy maintains the following sets of logical CPUs:

     9→
    10→Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11→distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→*/
    16→
    17→package topologymanager
    18→
    19→import (
    20→	"context"
    21→	"sync"
    22→
    23→	"k8s.io/api/core/v1"
    24→	"k8s.io/klog/v2"
    25→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/admission"
    26→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap"
    27→	"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle"
    28→)
    29→
    30→const (
    31→	// containerTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per container.
    32→	containerTopologyScope = "container"
    33→	// podTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per pod.
    34→	podTopologyScope = "pod"
    35→	// noneTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope when topologyPolicyName is none.
    36→	noneTopologyScope = "none"
    37→)
    38→
    39→type podTopologyHints map[string]map[string]TopologyHint
    40→
    41→// Scope interface for Topology Manager
    42→type Scope interface {
    43→	Name() string
    44→	GetPolicy() Policy
    45→	Admit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult
    46→	// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider
    47→	// wants to be consoluted with when making topology hints
    48→	AddHintProvider(h HintProvider)
    49→	// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking
    50→	AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)
    51→	// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking
    52→	RemoveContainer(containerID string) error
    53→	// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints
    54→	Store
    55→}
    56→
    57→type scope struct {
    58→	mutex sync.Mutex
    59→	name  string
    60→	// Mapping of a Pods mapping of Containers and their TopologyHints
    61→	// Indexed by PodUID to ContainerName
    62→	podTopologyHints podTopologyHints
    63→	// The list of components registered with the Manager
    64→	hintProviders []HintProvider
    65→	// Topology Manager Policy
    66→	policy Policy
    67→	// Mapping of (PodUid, ContainerName) to ContainerID for Adding/Removing Pods from PodTopologyHints mapping
    68→	podMap containermap.ContainerMap
    69→}
    70→
    71→func (s *scope) Name() string {
    72→	return s.name
    73→}
    74→
    75→func (s *scope) getTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {
    76→	s.mutex.Lock()
    77→	defer s.mutex.Unlock()
    78→	return s.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName]
    79→}
    80→
    81→func (s *scope) setTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string, th TopologyHint) {
    82→	s.mutex.Lock()
    83→	defer s.mutex.Unlock()
    84→
    85→	if s.podTopologyHints[podUID] == nil {
    86→		s.podTopologyHints[podUID] = make(map[string]TopologyHint)
    87→	}
    88→	s.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName] = th
    89→}
    90→
    91→func (s *scope) GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {
    92→	return s.getTopologyHints(podUID, containerName)
    93→}
    94→
    95→func (s *scope) GetPolicy() Policy {
    96→	return s.policy
    97→}
    98→
    99→func (s *scope) AddHintProvider(h HintProvider) {
   100→	s.hintProviders = append(s.hintProviders, h)
   101→}
   102→
   103→// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope
   104→// but topologymanager do not track mapping anymore
   105→func (s *scope) AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {
   106→	s.mutex.Lock()
   107→	defer s.mutex.Unlock()
   108→
   109→	s.podMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)
   110→}
   111→
   112→// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope
   113→// but topologymanager do not track mapping anymore
   114→func (s *scope) RemoveContainer(containerID string) error {
   115→	// Use context.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper context to pass in.
   116→	// Replace this with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a context parameter.
   117→	ctx := context.TODO()
   118→	logger := klog.FromContext(ctx)
   119→	s.mutex.Lock()
   120→	defer s.mutex.Unlock()
   121→
   122→	logger.Info("RemoveContainer", "containerID", containerID)
   123→	// Get the podUID and containerName associated with the containerID to be removed and remove it
   124→	podUIDString, containerName, err := s.podMap.GetContainerRef(containerID)
   125→	if err != nil {
   126→		return nil
   127→	}
   128→	s.podMap.RemoveByContainerID(containerID)
   129→
   130→	// In cases where a container has been restarted, it's possible that the same podUID and
   131→	// containerName are already associated with a *different* containerID now. Only remove
   132→	// the TopologyHints associated with that podUID and containerName if this is not true
   133→	if _, err := s.podMap.GetContainerID(podUIDString, containerName); err != nil {
   134→		delete(s.podTopologyHints[podUIDString], containerName)
   135→		if len(s.podTopologyHints[podUIDString]) == 0 {
   136→			delete(s.podTopologyHints, podUIDString)
   137→		}
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	return nil
   141→}
   142→
   143→func (s *scope) admitPolicyNone(pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {
   144→	for _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {
   145→		err := s.allocateAlignedResources(pod, &container)
   146→		if err != nil {
   147→			return admission.GetPodAdmitResult(err)
   148→		}
   149→	}
   150→	return admission.GetPodAdmitResult(nil)
   151→}
   152→
   153→// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope
   154→// but topologymanager do not track providers anymore
   155→func (s *scope) allocateAlignedResources(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error {
   156→	for _, provider := range s.hintProviders {
   157→		err := provider.Allocate(pod, container)
   158→		if err != nil {
   159→			return err
   160→		}
   161→	}
   162→	return nil
   163→}

(\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// containerTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per container.\n\tcontainerTopologyScope = \"container\"\n\t// podTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per pod.\n\tpodTopologyScope = \"pod\"\n\t// noneTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope when topologyPolicyName is none.\n\tnoneTopologyScope = \"none\"\n)\n\ntype podTopologyHints map[string]map[string]TopologyHint\n\n// Scope interface for Topology Manager\ntype Scope interface {\n\tName() string\n\tGetPolicy() Policy\n\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult\n\t// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider\n\t// wants to be consoluted with when making topology hints\n\tAddHintProvider(h HintProvider)\n\t// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking\n\tAddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\n\t// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking\n\tRemoveContainer(containerID string) error\n\t// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints\n\tStore\n}\n\ntype scope struct {\n\tmutex sync.Mutex\n\tname  string\n\t// Mapping of a Pods mapping of Containers and their TopologyHints\n\t// Indexed by PodUID to ContainerName\n\tpodTopologyHints podTopologyHints\n\t// The list of components registered with the Manager\n\thintProviders []HintProvider\n\t// Topology Manager Policy\n\tpolicy Policy\n\t// Mapping of (PodUid, ContainerName) to ContainerID for Adding/Removing Pods from PodTopologyHints mapping\n\tpodMap containermap.ContainerMap\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) Name() string {\n\treturn s.name\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) getTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {\n\ts.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn s.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName]\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) setTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string, th TopologyHint) {\n\ts.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif s.podTopologyHints[podUID] == nil {\n\t\ts.podTopologyHints[podUID] = make(map[string]TopologyHint)\n\t}\n\ts.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName] = th\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {\n\treturn s.getTopologyHints(podUID, containerName)\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) GetPolicy() Policy {\n\treturn s.policy\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) AddHintProvider(h HintProvider) {\n\ts.hintProviders = append(s.hintProviders, h)\n}\n\n// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope\n// but topologymanager do not track mapping anymore\nfunc (s *scope) AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {\n\ts.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\ts.podMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)\n}\n\n// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope\n// but topologymanager do not track mapping anymore\nfunc (s *scope) RemoveContainer(containerID string) error {\n\t// Use context.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper context to pass in.\n\t// Replace this with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a context parameter.\n\tctx := context.TODO()\n\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n\ts.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tlogger.Info(\"RemoveContainer\", \"containerID\", containerID)\n\t// Get the podUID and containerName associated with the containerID to be removed and remove it\n\tpodUIDString, containerName, err := s.podMap.GetContainerRef(containerID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\ts.podMap.RemoveByContainerID(containerID)\n\n\t// In cases where a container has been restarted, it's possible that the same podUID and\n\t// containerName are already associated with a *different* containerID now. Only remove\n\t// the TopologyHints associated with that podUID and containerName if this is not true\n\tif _, err := s.podMap.GetContainerID(podUIDString, containerName); err != nil {\n\t\tdelete(s.podTopologyHints[podUIDString], containerName)\n\t\tif len(s.podTopologyHints[podUIDString]) == 0 {\n\t\t\tdelete(s.podTopologyHints, podUIDString)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *scope) admitPolicyNone(pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {\n\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {\n\t\terr := s.allocateAlignedResources(pod, &container)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn admission.GetPodAdmitResult(err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn admission.GetPodAdmitResult(nil)\n}\n\n// It would be better to implement this function in topologymanager instead of scope\n// but topologymanager do not track providers anymore\nfunc (s *scope) allocateAlignedResources(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error {\n\tfor _, provider := range s.hintProviders {\n\t\terr := provider.Allocate(pod, container)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n", "numLines": 164, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 164}}

   111→// NewManager creates a new DRA manager.

   113→// Most errors returned by the manager show up in the context of a pod.

   145→	return manager, nil
   146→}

80→// The static policy maintains the following sets of logical CPUs:
    81→//
    82→//   - SHARED: Burstable, BestEffort, and non-integral Guaranteed containers
    83→//     run here. Initially this contains all CPU IDs on the system. As
    84→//     exclusive allocations are created and destroyed, this CPU set shrinks
    85→//     and grows, accordingly. This is stored in the state as the default
    86→//     CPU set.
    87→//
    88→//   - RESERVED: A subset of the shared pool which is not exclusively
    89→//     allocatable. The membership of this pool is static for the lifetime of
    90→//     the Kubelet. The size of the reserved pool is
    91→//     ceil(systemreserved.cpu + kubereserved.cpu).
    92→//     Reserved CPUs are taken topologically starting with lowest-indexed
    93→//     physical core, as reported by cAdvisor.
    94→//
    95→//   - ASSIGNABLE: Equal to SHARED - RESERVED. Exclusive CPUs are allocated
    96→//     from this pool.
    97→//
    98→//   - EXCLUSIVE ALLOCATIONS: CPU sets assigned exclusively to one container.
    99→//     These are stored as explicit assignments in the state.
   100→//
   101→// When an exclusive allocation is made, the static policy also updates the

This is stored in the state as the default\n//     CPU set.\n//\n//   - RESERVED: A subset of the shared pool which is not exclusively\n//     allocatable.
